stories.title: **Alabama vs. Tennessee Game 3 Tonight — SEC Title Implications on the Line**

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Alabama crushed Tennessee 12-0 Saturday.
Tennessee shut out Alabama 2-0 Sunday.

Tonight, Game 3. 7 PM ET. SEC Network.

The winner controls their SEC title fate. The loser watches from the sideline.

#RollTide #GoBigOrange #SECSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Alabama (44-5, 16-4 SEC) is 1.5 games behind No. 1 Oklahoma in the SEC race.

A loss tonight to Tennessee doesn't just sting — it likely ends Alabama's SEC title hopes.

Game 3. Tonight. 7 PM ET. SEC Network.

#SECSoftball #RollTide

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

🥎 THE RUBBER MATCH IS TONIGHT — AND THE STAKES COULDN'T BE HIGHER

The Alabama-Tennessee series couldn't have been more dramatic if you scripted it.

Saturday: Alabama crushed Tennessee 12-0 in five innings. Jocelyn Briski was dominant in the circle, and the Crimson Tide looked every bit like the No. 3 team in the country.

Sunday: Tennessee answered with a masterpiece. Sage Mardjetko (now 11-1 on the season) held Alabama to one hit through seven innings, shutting out the Crimson Tide 2-0 in what was Alabama's first shutout of the entire 2026 season.

Now comes Game 3.

Tonight. 7 PM ET. SEC Network. Knoxville, Tennessee.

What's at stake: Alabama (44-5, 16-4 SEC) enters Game 3 sitting 1.5 games behind No. 1 Oklahoma (45-6, 17-3 SEC) in the SEC standings. With just six conference games left in the regular season, a loss tonight would essentially end Alabama's hopes of winning the SEC title outright. A win keeps the pressure on Oklahoma and sets up a thrilling final weekend.

For Tennessee (ranked No. 8), a series win over No. 3 Alabama would be a massive statement heading into the SEC tournament and could vault the Lady Vols into the top-5 in this week's NFCA poll.

Who starts in the circle? That's the big question. Tennessee could go back to Mardjetko on short rest given Sunday's masterclass. Alabama will need its best from Jocelyn Briski or Vic Moten in a winner-take-all environment.

This is the best series in college softball right now — and it ends tonight.

What's your prediction? Drop it below! 👇

#RollTide #GoBigOrange #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

---

stories.title: **Kendall Wells One Home Run From NCAA All-Time Record**

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells has 36 home runs.

The all-time NCAA single-season record: 37 (Lauren Espinoza, Arizona, 1995).

One swing from history. The 31-year record is RIGHT THERE.

#Sooners #OUSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells is a freshman.

She already:
✅ Broke the NCAA freshman HR record (No. 31)
✅ Leads No. 1 Oklahoma with 36 homers
✅ Has OU one swing from the all-time single-season record

One home run from doing what no one in 31 years has done.

#Sooners #OUSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

🏠 ONE HOME RUN FROM HISTORY

Lauren Espinoza set the all-time NCAA single-season home run record with 37 home runs for Arizona in 1995 — 31 years ago.

No one has touched it since.

Until now, maybe.

Oklahoma freshman Kendall Wells hit her 36th home run of the season Sunday in the Sooners' 3-1 win over No. 11 Georgia. Just one home run separates her from the most celebrated record in college softball history.

What makes this even more remarkable: Wells already owns the NCAA freshman home run record (HR No. 31, April 11 vs. Texas). She's doing things no first-year player has ever done, in a season that has No. 1 Oklahoma at 45-6 and firmly in control of their national championship destiny.

If Wells hits No. 37, she'll join Espinoza as the only player in Division I softball history to hit that many in a single season. And she'll do it as a freshman — someone who hadn't played a single college game before February.

Oklahoma still has five regular-season games remaining, plus conference and postseason play. Wells has plenty of chances.

The record is right there.

Will she get it?

#Sooners #OUSoftball #CollegeSoftball #MakeHistory

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

36 home runs. One away from the all-time NCAA record (37, Espinoza, 1995). Freshman. Kendall Wells is doing something this sport has never seen. 🏠🥎 #Sooners #OUSoftball #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: **Katie Stewart Breaks Texas Program HR Record in Kentucky Sweep**

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Katie Stewart just broke the Texas program's single-season home run record.

24 home runs this season.

She broke a record set just last year and did it at the site of the upcoming SEC Tournament.

#HookEm #LonghornSoftball #SECSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

🤘 KATIE STEWART MAKES LONGHORN HISTORY

Junior first baseman Katie Stewart had a weekend for the record books at Kentucky — and we mean that literally.

On Saturday, Stewart crushed a go-ahead grand slam in the seventh inning to tie the Texas program's single-season home run record at 23, helping the Longhorns win 6-1.

Then on Sunday, she went one better — hitting home run No. 24 to break Reese Atwood's Texas program record outright. Texas finished the game 11-2 and swept the series from Kentucky.

What makes Stewart's feat even sweeter: she broke a record that was set just last year (Atwood hit 23 in 2025). And she did it with postseason games still ahead — giving her the chance to push the record even higher before her junior season is done.

No. 4 Texas (approximately 40-9 overall, 14-7 SEC) is firmly in national seed contention, and Stewart's power surge has been a major reason why. She leads the SEC in home runs among juniors and is one of the most feared hitters in the country right now.

We said after March that Stewart was someone to watch. She's delivering.

#HookEm #LonghornSoftball #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Katie Stewart: 24 home runs. A new Texas program record. And she still has postseason games to add to the total. 🤘 #HookEm #LonghornSoftball #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: **Nebraska 14-Game Win Streak — Huskers Eye 40 Wins and Big Ten Title**

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nebraska is 39-6.
On a 14-game win streak.
One win from 40 on the season.
19-1 in the Big Ten.

Sunday's game got rained out — so win No. 40 will have to wait.

But it IS coming.

#Huskers #GBR #BigTenSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM ET

🌽 NEBRASKA IS ROLLING — AND WIN NO. 40 IS JUST AROUND THE CORNER

The Nebraska Huskers had their momentum briefly slowed Sunday — but not by an opponent. Weather forced the cancellation of the series finale against Iowa, leaving the Huskers at 39-6 and sitting on a 14-game win streak.

Still, the numbers from this weekend were impressive. Nebraska swept Friday's doubleheader against Iowa 9-1 and 5-2, drawing a program-record crowd of 3,396 to watch the twin bill. The Huskers are 19-1 in Big Ten play — essentially a lock for the Big Ten title.

Earlier this month, Nebraska outscored Minnesota 32-4 in a weekend sweep (including a 17-2 run-rule win), which vaulted the Huskers to No. 1 in the Softball America poll. They've won 25 of their last 26 games.

Win No. 40 looms. The 40-win threshold is a marker of elite college softball programs, and Nebraska is one game away.

Next up: the Huskers will look to punch that milestone before conference tournament play begins. With a Big Ten title in hand and a potential top-4 national seed in range, Nebraska has quietly become one of the most dangerous teams heading into May.

Don't sleep on the Huskers.

#Huskers #GBR #BigTenSoftball #CollegeSoftball

---

stories.title: **UCLA Extends Hot Streak with Series Win vs. No. 25 Washington**

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
No. 6 UCLA has won 12 of its last 13 games.

Sunday: 7-2 over No. 25 Washington on the road.

The Bruins are quietly building one of the best late-season résumés in the country.

#GoBruins #UCLASoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:30 PM ET

🥎 UCLA IS PEAKING AT THE PERFECT TIME

No. 6 UCLA's 7-2 win over No. 25 Washington on Sunday isn't just another result — it's the latest data point in one of the most impressive late-season runs in college softball.

The Bruins have won 12 of their last 13 games, including a series win over a ranked opponent on the road in Seattle. UCLA's offense has been clicking, their pitching has been consistent, and the timing couldn't be better with conference tournament play just days away.

The Bruins are squarely in the top-8 national seed conversation, which would give them home hosting rights for the NCAA Regional. That's a significant advantage in the postseason — and UCLA's recent form makes a compelling case for it.

If there's a team flying under the radar while Oklahoma, Alabama, Texas Tech, and Nebraska grab the headlines — it's UCLA right now.

Keep an eye on the Bruins in May.

#GoBruins #UCLASoftball #CollegeSoftball #BigTenSoftball

---

stories.title: **NFCA Week 13 Poll Drops Today — Wild Weekend Reshuffles Rankings**

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
The NFCA Week 13 poll drops today.

Last week: Oklahoma No. 1, Texas Tech No. 2, Alabama No. 3.

This weekend Alabama was shut out by Tennessee. Nebraska won its 14th straight. Texas swept Kentucky.

The Nos. 2-5 picture is about to get interesting.

#CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 PM ET

📊 NFCA WEEK 13 POLL DROPS TODAY — WHO MOVES?

The NFCA/GoRout Division I Top 25 Coaches Poll for Week 13 is out today, and after one of the wildest weekends of the season, the top-5 picture is worth examining.

Here's what the poll voters have to process:

▶️ Alabama was shut out by Tennessee 2-0 Sunday (the Tide's first shutout of the season) and trails OU by 1.5 games in the SEC with a rubber match tonight
▶️ Nebraska won its 14th straight game and is 19-1 in the Big Ten
▶️ Texas swept Kentucky with Katie Stewart setting a program home run record
▶️ UCLA has won 12 of 13 and is rising fast

Last week (April 21): Oklahoma No. 1 | Texas Tech No. 2 | Alabama No. 3

Oklahoma's No. 1 spot is safe — the Sooners are 45-6 with Wells one homer from the all-time record. But everything below No. 1 is in flux.

Nebraska's case for No. 2 or No. 3 is growing. Alabama's shutout performance Sunday may cost them a spot or two. Texas Tech and Florida remain firmly in the mix.

We'll update our coverage as the poll numbers come in.

#CollegeSoftball #NFCApoll #SoftballRankings

---

stories.title: **Conference Tournament Countdown — SEC in Lexington Starting May 5**

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
The road to Oklahoma City starts in 3 days.

Conference tournaments begin April 30.

Selection Show: May 10, 7 PM ET ESPN2.
WCWS: May 28, Devon Park, Oklahoma City.

Who's your pick to cut down the net?

#WCWS #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 PM ET

🏆 THE ROAD TO OKC STARTS IN 3 DAYS — HERE'S YOUR 2026 WCWS GUIDE

Conference tournament season kicks off April 30, and here's your complete guide to the road to the Women's College World Series.

📅 KEY DATES:
• April 30 — Conference tournaments begin (various)
• May 5-10 — SEC Tournament, John Cropp Stadium, Lexington, KY (15-team single-elimination)
• May 10, 7 PM ET (ESPN2) — WCWS Selection Show
• May 15 — NCAA Regional play begins (16 sites)
• May 28 — WCWS opens, Devon Park, Oklahoma City

🆕 NEW IN 2026: The NCAA tournament field seeds 32 teams for the first time (up from 16), which means more programs receive seeded placement and home hosting advantage. With 31 automatic conference bids and 33 at-large spots, the field is 64 teams total.

The power programs (Oklahoma, Texas Tech, Alabama, Nebraska, Texas, UCLA) are competing for national seeds that guarantee hosting rights in regionals and super regionals.

Oklahoma City. Devon Park. May 28. 

Who's going to OKC?

#WCWS #CollegeSoftball #RoadToOKC #WomensCollegeWorldSeries
